findings.evidence: The close-third focal pressure drops out for long hearing stretches at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:41 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:61 and again at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:159 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:179, with Halima largely reduced to reporting speech, crowd motion, and rulings until the narration re-enters her at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:63 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:181.
findings.problem: This chapter's power depends on Halima paying for every revelation, but two 10+ paragraph runs let the scene operate as procedural camera instead of close-third fiction. The hearing remains legible, yet the personal cost briefly goes abstract right where the chapter should feel most expensive.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the stretches beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:41 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:159 so Halima's mind and body stay in the scene. Add brief but specific interior beats tied to the cases already on the table: bodily stress, involuntary memory, judgment, or self-correction when the aunt is reclaimed, when Jamila names the debt-marriage threat, and when Majed gives the house answer. Keep the case order, testimony content, and rulings unchanged.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:41 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:61 and again between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:159 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:179, no run exceeds eight consecutive paragraphs without an explicit Halima thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment. Fail if either block still reads as externally observed testimony only.

findings.evidence: The chapter repeats the same simile scaffold three times at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:141 ("like a man chewing sand"), snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:331 ("like a man receiving a private blessing"), and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:347 ("like a man tightening a bolt...").
findings.problem: Once or twice this frame reads as intentional sentence music; by the third use it becomes a visible template. The repetition is especially noticeable because each instance lands on a major emotional beat, so the prose starts to sound assembled from a reusable comparison pattern rather than freshly perceived.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise at least two of the comparison beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:331, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:347. Replace the repeated "like a man ..." starter with images drawn from each local moment's actual materials and pressures: Kashem's speech under appeal, Mofiz's petty triumph, or Majed's mechanic's hands and failing threads.
findings.acceptance_test: Pass if no more than one of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:141, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:331, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_15.md:347 still uses the "like a man ..." construction, and the revised lines produce three distinct image sources tied to those specific moments. Fail if two or more of those beats still share the same scaffold.

summary: Chapter 15 clears the structural bar: the hearing has real causal consequence, the boundary beats land, character voices stay distinct, the prose stays in past tense, and I found no MEDIUM+ continuity contradiction, sanitization drift, timidity, or example-line leakage from the style bible. It still fails on craft and prose because the close-third pressure drops out for extended courtroom-camera stretches and a repeated "like a man ..." simile frame becomes a prose tic at key turns.
